name: Vevchani Carnival - Tradition old 1400 years
description: One of the most famous village festivals in the Balkans. Over 1,400 years old, based on old Pagan beliefs and rituals. The carnival is the ritual of calling after Saint Basil the Great, coinciding with the Twelve Days of Orthodox Christmas and the Orthodox New Year. Participants ('Vasiličari') disguise in elaborate masks expressing humor and sarcasm — devils, demons, mythical characters. Traditional music on zurlas and drums. Became part of the World Federation of Carnival Cities in 1993. A special Carnival Passport is issued. The Carnival is a mix of pagan customs translated into modern language, uniting all forms of expression of traditional culture.

name: Carnival of Binche
dates: Three days before Shrove Tuesday (annual, February/March)
description: UNESCO-recognized Masterpiece of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity. Dating back to the 14th century. Characterized by the Gilles — costumed performers wearing wax masks and ostrich feather headdresses who throw oranges into the crowd as a symbol of good luck. Highlights the town's rich cultural heritage and preserves ancestral traditions.

name: Kukeri Rituals
city: Various (nationwide, major events in Pernik and Shiroka Laka)
description: Ancient pagan tradition performed to chase away evil spirits and bring good fortune and fertility. Participants wear elaborate costumes made of fur and bells, parade through the streets performing rhythmic dances and rituals. A captivating display of folkloric traditions preserving the rich cultural heritage of Bulgaria's rural communities. Featured as a prime example of living European heritage by the European Heritage Days programme.
